role_notes: PPP GB president and former GBA-1 member. Won this seat in 2020 by a 2822-vote margin and is the verified PPP face for 2026.
Former GB chief minister (2015 to 2020). PML-N anchor figure in Gilgit-II per the 2026 GB Assembly election article.
2020 GBA-2 winner under PTI (margin of 4 votes). Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
2020 GBA-3 winner under PTI. Wikipedia profile lists party as PPP for 2026.
2020 GBA-5 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
LA-7 Skardu-1 nominee. Son of former GB chief minister Syed Mehdi Shah (who contested this seat in 2009 won 2015 3rd 2020 3rd). The 2026 candidate is the son not the father.
2020 GBA-8 winner under MWM. Wikipedia profile confirms MWM and GBA-8 for 2026.
2020 GBA-9 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists party as JUI-F for 2026.
2020 GBA-10 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists PPP from 2025 and constituency GBA-10.
2020 GBA-12 winner under PTI. Wikipedia profile lists party as TLP (Tehreek-e-Labbaik Pakistan) for 2026.
2020 GBA-14 winner under PTI (listed in 2020 records as Shamsul Haq). Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
2020 GBA-15 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
Current chief minister of Gilgit-Baltistan (took office through the 2023 post-disqualification rearrangement). 2020 GBA-18 winner under PTI. Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
2020 GBA-20 winner under PTI. Wikipedia profile says he joined PPP on 24 April 2026 and lists constituency GBA-20.
2020 GBA-22 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
